event: shifted to medical intensive care unit
broad-spectrum antibiotic and inotropic support (noradrenaline) was started
kidney function tests were highly deranged
liver function tests were highly deranged
hematological parameters were sent to the laboratory
coagulation parameters were sent to the laboratory
serum triglyceride was sent to the laboratory
serum fibrinogen level was sent to the laboratory
serum Ferritin was sent to the laboratory
bone marrow biopsy sample was taken
LFT, KFT, and coagulation profile were grossly deranged
bone marrow biopsy demonstrated presence of hemophagocytosis in the bone marrow
switched to IV dexamethasone as maintenance treatment
TPE procedures were done aggressively on 3 consecutive days
TPE was done using COM.TEC (Fresenius Kabi, Germany)
written consent was taken from the father
benefits and risks of TPE were explained to him in detail
one standard TPE procedure was 1.5 plasma volume exchanges
fresh frozen plasma (FFP) was used as a replacement fluid
peripheral femoral line was used for TPE
required blood transfusion of 2 units of packed red cells
required FFP (used during plasma exchange) - 18 units
required single donor platelet concentrates - 4 units
hematological, biochemical, and coagulation parameters were within normal range
complete blood count was done using XN 1000 (SYSMEX, USA)
biochemical parameters were done with Vitros 5600 (Ortho Clinical Diagnostics, USA)
coagulation parameters were measured on Destiny Plus (Tcoag, Stago)
diagnosis was made based on the HLH 2004 criteria
